---
title: "Figure 4—figure supplement 3 — the distortion split into sample and correlation: R's floor is all correlation"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
THE MECHANISM BEHIND FIGURE 4'S HEADLINE. Figure 4 shows that more channels do not rescue R: its
k_off distortion sits on a flat floor near 1.44 across four decades while IR converges to 1. This
supplement says WHY, by splitting the total information distortion into the two factors the machinery
already computes: a per-sample (Gaussian-approximation) part and a temporal-correlation part.

Measured, k_off, noise 0.1 (medians over the 7 intervals):
                total  sample  corr
  R  N_ch 10    1.42   1.086   1.35
  R  N_ch 100   1.49   1.071   1.36
  R  N_ch 1000  1.48   1.024   1.43
  R  N_ch 10000 1.46   0.998   1.47   <- sample gone to 1, ALL of it is correlation
  IR N_ch 10    1.37   1.099   1.24
  IR N_ch 10000 1.01   1.007   1.00

So R and IR do NOT differ in per-sample fidelity: BOTH sample parts go to 1 as the channel count
grows, i.e. the per-sample Gaussian approximation becomes exact for both, as it must. What does not
vanish for R is the CORRELATION part, which holds at 1.35-1.47 and even grows. R's 1.44 floor IS the
correlation term. That is the mechanism: conditioning on the interval buys temporal decorrelation,
not per-sample accuracy, which is also why more channels cannot fix R. This corroborates, by an
independent route, the score-autocorrelation contrast already in Figure 3 (IR white, non-recursive
strongly correlated).

WHY R AND IR TOGETHER, NOT ONE SUPPLEMENT EACH. The finding is the COMPARISON of the two
decompositions - both sample parts vanish, only R's correlation survives. Split into two figures it
disappears, the same way the IR-only map disappeared into Figure 4's rows.

THE MULTIPLICATIVE READING. The exact relation is a congruence, GIDM = K.CDM.K', not a product, so
total = sample x correlation is NOT algebra. But measured over the whole volume it holds to a median
0.04% and a max 2.9% on the diagonal, so the three maps can be read multiplicatively AS AN EMPIRICAL
NEAR-IDENTITY with that bound - stated in the caption, not asserted as exact. The exactly additive
statement is log det GIDM = log det GSDM + log det CDM (1e-14). Do NOT use
Likelihood_Information_Distortion_Reconstituted: it implements the false product identity and is NaN.

DATA: battery_pool_G (theta_pool), Gaussian family. k_off only, the direction that carries the
failure (figure_4_supplement_1 shows the others). Standard 4 N_ch x 3 noise grid. No new runs.

TRAP: the "noise" label is not the physical noise. current_noise = label/1000.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse); library(patchwork)
```

```{r params}
ALGOS    <- c("macro_R", "macro_IR")
ALGO_LAB <- c(macro_R = "R", macro_IR = "IR")
NCHS   <- c(10, 100, 1000, 10000)
NOISES <- c("0.1", "1", "10")
DATA_DIRS <- c("../data/1c2ae6f", "../data/87889e6")

POFF  <- 1                                   # k_off, the direction that carries the failure
COMPS <- c(total  = "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Gaussian_Information_Distortion",
           sample = "Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Sample_Distortion",
           corr   = "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Correlation_Distortion")
COMP_LAB <- c(total = "total", sample = "sample", corr = "correlation")

RAMP <- c("#021024","#053061","#2166AC","#4393C3","#92C5DE","#D1E5F0","#F7F7F7",
          "#FDDBC7","#F4A582","#D6604D","#B2182B","#67001F","#3d0011")
bandcols <- function(breaks) {
  n <- length(breaks) - 1; ctr <- findInterval(0, breaks); nb <- ctr - 1; nr <- n - ctr; K <- max(nb, nr, 1)
  blue <- colorRampPalette(rev(RAMP[1:7]), space = "Lab")(K + 1)
  red  <- colorRampPalette(RAMP[7:13],     space = "Lab")(K + 1)
  cols <- rep(RAMP[7], n)
  if (nb > 0) cols[seq(ctr - 1, 1)] <- blue[2:(nb + 1)]
  if (nr > 0) cols[seq(ctr + 1, n)] <- red[2:(nr + 1)]
  cols
}
fmt_big <- function(x) formatC(x, format = "g", digits = 3)
DIST_LIN    <- c(0.5, 0.67, 0.8, 0.87, 0.91, 0.94, 0.97, 0.99, 1.01, 1.03, 1.06, 1.1, 1.15, 1.25, 1.5, 1.7, 2.5)
DIST_BREAKS <- log10(DIST_LIN); DIST_PAL <- bandcols(DIST_BREAKS); DIST_LAB <- fmt_big(DIST_LIN)
```

```{r data}
findf <- function(nch, algo, z) {
  f <- file.path(DATA_DIRS, sprintf("figure_3_G_nch_%d_nsim_10000_%s_noise_%s_battery_pool_G.csv", nch, algo, z))
  hit <- f[file.exists(f)]
  if (!length(hit)) stop("supp3: no file for ", algo, " N_ch ", nch, " noise ", z)
  hit[1]
}
cells <- expand_grid(algo = ALGOS, nch = NCHS, z = NOISES)
cat("supp3:", nrow(cells), "cells, k_off, split into", paste(names(COMPS), collapse = "/"), "\n")

stat <- pmap_dfr(cells, function(algo, nch, z)
    read.csv(findf(nch, algo, z), skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% mutate(noise = z, algo = algo)) %>%
  filter(component_path %in% COMPS, param_index == param_col, param_index == POFF,
         statistic == "value", probit %in% c("mean", "quantile")) %>%
  mutate(comp = names(COMPS)[match(component_path, COMPS)],
         key  = case_when(probit == "mean" ~ "m", quantile_level == 0.025 ~ "lo",
                          quantile_level == 0.975 ~ "hi", TRUE ~ NA_character_)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(key)) %>%
  group_by(comp, algo, noise, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, key) %>%
  summarise(v = mean(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = key, values_from = v) %>%
  mutate(conf = ifelse(m > 1, pmax(1, lo), pmin(1, hi)),
         lx = log10(interval_in_tau), ly = log10(as.numeric(noise)))

# verify the multiplicative near-identity ON THIS DATA, and print the bound the caption quotes
mult <- stat %>% select(comp, algo, noise, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, m) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = comp, values_from = m) %>%
  mutate(rel_err = abs(sample * corr - total) / total)
cat(sprintf("multiplicative reading total ~ sample x corr: median %.3f%%, 90th %.3f%%, max %.3f%%\n",
    100 * median(mult$rel_err, na.rm = TRUE), 100 * quantile(mult$rel_err, .9, na.rm = TRUE),
    100 * max(mult$rel_err, na.rm = TRUE)))

sat <- function(v) cat(sprintf("  distortion  %d/%d below %.3g, %d/%d above %.3g\n",
  sum(log10(v) < min(DIST_BREAKS), na.rm = TRUE), sum(is.finite(v)), min(DIST_LIN),
  sum(log10(v) > max(DIST_BREAKS), na.rm = TRUE), sum(is.finite(v)), max(DIST_LIN)))
cat("scale saturation:\n"); sat(stat$conf)
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 12}
X_BRK <- log10(c(.01, .1, 1)); Y_BRK <- log10(c(.1, 1, 10))

# rows: component (outer) then algorithm (inner). total, sample, correlation x R, IR = 6 rows.
COMP_ORD <- names(COMPS)
rowfac <- function(d) {
  lv <- as.vector(t(outer(COMP_ORD, ALGOS,
          function(c, a) sprintf("atop(\"%s\", bold(\"%s\"))", COMP_LAB[c], ALGO_LAB[a]))))
  d %>% mutate(prow = factor(sprintf("atop(\"%s\", bold(\"%s\"))", COMP_LAB[comp], ALGO_LAB[algo]),
                             levels = lv),
               nchf = factor(paste0("N[ch]==", Num_ch), levels = paste0("N[ch]==", NCHS)))
}

d <- rowfac(stat) %>% mutate(zz = pmin(pmax(log10(conf), min(DIST_BREAKS)), max(DIST_BREAKS)))
g <- ggplot(d, aes(lx, ly, z = zz)) +
  geom_contour_filled(breaks = DIST_BREAKS) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = DIST_PAL, drop = FALSE, guide = "none") +
  geom_point(size = .08, colour = "grey35", alpha = .35) +
  facet_grid(prow ~ nchf, labeller = label_parsed) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = X_BRK, labels = c(".01", ".1", "1")) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = Y_BRK, labels = c(".1", "1", "10")) +
  labs(x = expression(Delta %.% k[off]), y = "noise",
       title = "k[off] information distortion, split total = sample x correlation") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 8, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.spacing = unit(0.1, "cm"),
        legend.position = "none", axis.text = element_text(size = 6),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 8), strip.text.y = element_text(size = 6.5, angle = 0),
        strip.text.x = element_text(size = 7.5), plot.title = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2))

cbar <- {
  n <- length(DIST_PAL); pos <- function(v) match(v, DIST_LIN) - 0.5
  ggplot(data.frame(x = seq_len(n)), aes(x, 1, fill = factor(x))) +
    geom_tile(colour = "grey92", linewidth = .15) +
    annotate("segment", x = pos(c(0.87, 1.15)), xend = pos(c(0.87, 1.15)), y = 0.5, yend = 2,
             colour = "black", linewidth = .5) +
    annotate("segment", x = pos(c(0.67, 1.5)), xend = pos(c(0.67, 1.5)), y = 0.5, yend = 2,
             colour = "grey25", linewidth = .35, linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = DIST_PAL, guide = "none") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0.5, n + 0.5, 1), labels = DIST_LAB, expand = c(0, 0)) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.5, 2.0), clip = "off") + labs(y = "distortion") +
    theme_void(base_family = "Helvetica") +
    theme(axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, size = 7, hjust = 1, vjust = .5, margin = margin(r = 4)),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 5.5), axis.ticks.x = element_line(colour = "grey55", linewidth = .3),
          axis.ticks.length.x = unit(2, "pt"), plot.margin = margin(3, 26, 2, 34))
}
gg <- g / cbar + plot_layout(heights = c(6 * 0.95, 0.3))
H <- 6 * 0.95 + 0.3 + 0.6
gg
ggsave("Figure_4_supplement_3.pdf", gg, width = 7.0, height = H)
cat("Figure_4_supplement_3.pdf: 6 rows, height", round(H, 1), "in\n")
```

```{r caption-numbers}
cat("\n=== supp 3 caption numbers ===\n")
# RAW medians (median of the mean estimate over intervals), NOT the CI-shrunk value the map colours.
# The quoted decomposition values match Figure 4-figure supplement 4 and decisions.md (which are raw);
# the map itself colours the conservative CI-shrunk bound `conf`, so a cell near one reads slightly
# closer to one in colour than the number printed here.
cat("\nk_off split, RAW medians over the 7 intervals, noise 0.1 (map colours the CI-shrunk bound)\n")
print(as.data.frame(stat %>% filter(noise == "0.1") %>%
  group_by(algo, comp, Num_ch) %>% summarise(v = median(m), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(algo = ALGO_LAB[algo], comp = COMP_LAB[comp]) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Num_ch, values_from = v) %>%
  arrange(algo, match(comp, COMP_LAB))), digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nsample part -> 1 with N_ch (both algorithms); correlation is what survives for R\n")
print(as.data.frame(stat %>% filter(noise == "0.1", comp %in% c("sample", "corr")) %>%
  group_by(algo, comp, Num_ch) %>% summarise(v = median(m), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(algo = ALGO_LAB[algo]) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = comp, values_from = v) %>% arrange(algo, Num_ch)),
  digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)
```
